scenes.text: Seven trillion dollars in insurance reserves. Proven every year by handing over everything.
Regulators demand the full liability table — policy counts, benefit amounts, claim probabilities. Every reserve audit exposes confidential data. There is no privacy in today's actuarial compliance.
ZK Reserve changes that. A zero-knowledge proof that your vault is solvent — without revealing a single line of the liability table.

The circuit encodes the actuarial Expected Present Value formula across ten policy cohorts — term life, annuities, disability, critical illness — all in fixed-point arithmetic. The math runs in zero knowledge.
A Noir circuit takes the private liability table as witness and vault reserves as public input. It generates a compact UltraHonk proof — under four kilobytes — that reserve adequacy holds.

That proof is submitted to a Soroban smart contract on Stellar testnet. The verifier checks the UltraHonk proof using Stellar's native BN254 host functions — introduced in Protocol 26 — and stores the attestation permanently on-chain.
The result: an immutable attestation. Proof of solvency — auditable by anyone, exposing nothing to anyone.
Three-thousand-byte WASM contract. Ten actuarial cohorts. One proof. Deployed live on Stellar testnet.
ZK Reserve. Actuarial reserve attestation for the era of verifiable finance. Built for Stellar Hacks 2026.
